briefing.headline: Pitch it tight — pre-spawn stained water puts a jig in its best element.
briefing.gameplan:
In pre-spawn with stained water and post frontal conditions, jigs presentations shine.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.color: Alabama craw with double header trailer
primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, bluff, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep baits close to the bottom on ledges, but often burn them and kill it to trigger a strike — that retrieve variation produced key fish
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, bluff, riprap. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Three swimbait styles for different conditions: Bulkier hollow-body for slower presentations — great prespawn and stained water for extra vibration. Subtle finesse swimbaits (like Swimming Shiner) for clear water and tough bites on bright da...

Palaniuk: 'The spot had a riprap bank with a steep drop, then a trough that came up onto a hard bottom ridge, tapered back off into another trough. The trough made a horseshoe. A 40-yard section where the fish wanted to live, with a 25-foot hole t...
Wheeler: For a spring pre-spawn event limited to three rods, picks a jerkbait/crankbait for deeper staging fish, a 1/2-oz jig for versatility, and a ChatterBait for stained shallow water.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal pre-spawn fish pull back to 6-12ft staging areas. They won't commit shallow after the front. Target the first breakline off spawning flats and key on deeper structure near shallow spawning zones.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
